title: हनुमान लाङ्गूलास्त्र स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री हनुमान लाङ्गूलास्त्र स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्री हनुमल्लाङ्गूलास्त्रस्तोत्रस्य श्रीरामचन्द्र ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीहनुमान् देवता। मम सर्वभयनिवारणार्थे जपे विनियोगः।

ॐ ह्रीं ह्रौं हनुमते लाङ्गूलास्त्राय फट्।

ॐ नमो भगवते पञ्चवक्त्रहनूमते
प्रकटपराक्रमाय प्रबलबाहवे।
भूतप्रेतपिशाचब्रह्मराक्षसानपसारय अपसारय॥१॥

हनुमल्लाङ्गूलाग्निज्वालामालाविभूषितम्।
लङ्कापुरीदहनकं वन्दे लाङ्गूलवीरकम्॥२॥

यस्य लाङ्गूलपवने दह्यन्ते रावणालयाः।
तं हनूमन्तमुग्रं च लाङ्गूलास्त्रं नमाम्यहम्॥३॥

उद्यद्दिनकरसम्प्रभं महाबलपराक्रमम्।
लाङ्गूलेनाहतं शत्रून् हनूमन्तं नमाम्यहम्॥४॥

अग्निज्वालासमायुक्तं लाङ्गूलं यस्य शोभते।
तं वायुपुत्रमुग्रं च लाङ्गूलास्त्रं नमाम्यहम्॥५॥

शत्रून्सन्तापयामास लाङ्गूलेन महाबलः।
लङ्कां प्रदग्धवान्यस्तु तमहं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥६॥

लाङ्गूलास्त्रमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेन्नियतः शुचिः।
शत्रवो नश्यन्ति तस्य हनूमत्प्रसादतः॥७॥

॥ इति श्री हनुमान लाङ्गूलास्त्र स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Hanuman Langoolastra Stotram ||

Om Asya Shri Hanumal-Langoolastra-stotrasya Shri Ramachandra Rishih.
Anushtup Chhandah. Shri Hanuman Devata. Mama Sarva-bhaya-nivaranaarthe jape Viniyogah.

Om Hrim Hraum Hanumate Langoolastraya Phat.

Om Namo Bhagavate Pancha-vaktra-Hanumate
Prakata-parakramaya Prabala-bahave.
Bhuta-preta-pishacha-Brahma-rakshasaan apasaraya apasaraya.||1||

Hanumal-langula-agni-jwala-mala-vibhushitam.
Lanka-puri-dahanakam Vande Langula-virakam.||2||

Yasya Langula-pavane dahyante Ravanalayah.
Tam Hanumantam-ugram cha Langulastram namamyaham.||3||

Udyad-dinakara-samprabham Maha-bala-parakramam.
Langulenahatam shatrun Hanumantam namamyaham.||4||

Agni-jwala-samayuktam Langulam yasya shobhate.
Tam Vayu-putram-ugram cha Langulastram namamyaham.||5||

Shatrun santapayamasa Langulena Mahabalah.
Lankam pradagdhavan-yastu tam-aham pranamamy-aham.||6||

Langulastram-idam punyam yah pathen-niyatah shuchih.
Shatravo nashyanti tasya Hanumat-prasadatah.||7||

|| Iti Shri Hanuman Langoolastra Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: हनुमान लाङ्गूलास्त्र स्तोत्र में हनुमानजी की पूँछ (लाङ्गूल) को अस्त्र के रूप में वर्णित किया गया है। जिस पूँछ की अग्नि ज्वाला से लंका पुरी जल गई, उन उग्र हनुमानजी को नमस्कार है। इस स्तोत्र के पाठ से शत्रुओं का नाश होता है, भूत-प्रेत-पिशाच दूर भागते हैं और हनुमानजी की कृपा प्राप्त होती है।
benefits: Powerful protection against enemies and evil forces
Traditionally recited for removal of the effect of black magic and evil spirits
Invokes the fierce protective form of Hanuman
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears and provides courage
Burns away negativity like Lanka was burned
description: Hanuman Langoolastra Stotram — हनुमान लाङ्गूलास्त्र स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Hanuman. Rooted in Traditional, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to destroy the effect of black magic and evil spirits and remove all fears and provide courage. It is also said to burn away negativity like Lanka was burned. It is especially recommended on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Hanuman Jayanti for maximum spiritual benefit. With 7 verses and a reading time of about 7 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Hanuman Langoolastra Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Hanuman Langoolastra Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful prayer invoking Hanuman's tail-weapon for protection from enemies.
relatedSearches: Hanuman Langoolastra Stotram lyrics in Sanskrit
Hanuman protection mantra from black magic

seoFaqs.question: What does 'Langoolastra' mean in this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Langoola is the Sanskrit word for tail, and astra means weapon. Langoolastra Stotram therefore translates as 'the hymn of the tail-weapon,' invoking Hanuman in his fierce form as Veeranjaneya, who used his flaming tail to set Lanka ablaze in the Sundara Kanda of the Ramayana. The stotra meditates on the tail as a divine astra capable of repelling enemies, dispelling negative energies, and burning away karmic obstacles. (Source: Stotra Nidhi; Astromantra.)

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Hanuman Langoolastra Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional sources attribute the Langoolastra Stotra to a sage from the medieval period, though the exact rishi is not uniformly named across recensions. The composition is short, mantric in style, and has been preserved through oral and manuscript traditions, particularly in South India. It is widely chanted by Hanuman devotees and appears in many Sanskrit stotra anthologies, including those compiled in Telugu, Kannada, and Devanagari scripts. (Source: Stotra Rathna translations; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the prescribed sadhana for this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional manuals direct the sadhak to recite the Langoolastra Stotra one, three, seven, or twenty-one times while seated under a Peepal (Ashvattha) tree. The practice is repeated for forty-eight consecutive days while observing strict celibacy (brahmacharya), simple sattvic diet, and daily bath before recitation. By the grace of Maruti, traditional sources state, enemies and obstructions are mitigated. The stotra is also used as a daily protection mantra without the formal vrata. (Source: Astromantra; Bhaktinidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Langoolastra differ from Hanuman Chalisa or Bajrang Baan?
seoFaqs.answer: The Hanuman Chalisa is a 40-verse Awadhi hymn of praise composed by Tulsidas. Bajrang Baan is also attributed to Tulsidas and is a forceful invocation in Awadhi for urgent protection. The Langoolastra Stotram is a Sanskrit composition, shorter and more mantric in style, focused specifically on Hanuman's tail as a celestial weapon. Devotees typically recite the Chalisa for daily devotion, the Bajrang Baan for crisis intervention, and the Langoolastra as a structured 48-day sadhana for enemy-defence and removal of negative influences. (Source: Stotra Nidhi; Sonichits.)

seoFaqs.question: On which day should the Langoolastra Stotra be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Tuesday and Saturday are the traditional days for Hanuman worship, and the 48-day Langoolastra anushthana is typically begun on a Tuesday during Shukla Paksha (waxing moon). Devotees light a chameli oil or sesame oil lamp before a Hanuman image, offer red flowers and sindoor, and recite facing south or east. Many devotees from the southern part of India chant this Langoolastra Stotra daily as a general well-being mantra outside of formal sadhana. (Source: Hari Ome; Hindu Temples Guide.)
